capacity_evidence.target: two Blackhole P300c devices in a fixed 1x2 TP mesh
capacity_evidence.attention_partition: 32 of 64 Q heads and 4 of 8 KV heads per device; row-parallel O with one all-reduce
capacity_evidence.expert_partition: all 32 experts retain gate-selected top-4 execution; gate/up output and down K are fractured over TP-local intermediate 1440; down result is all-reduced; selected prefill program is 15/30 cores with 1x3 subblocks and selected decode program is 45/90 cores with 1x1 subblocks
capacity_evidence.validation: RUN_MULTICHIP_CONTEXT=1 test_full_context_cache_allocation_and_last_page_update allocates both paged local caches and executes a synchronized decode update at position 131071
capacity_evidence.prior_limit_status: superseded for this stage; TP2 KV-head and weight partitioning plus sparse active-expert execution fit the full HF-advertised cache contract

notes: The fixed 1x2 P300 path restores the HF-advertised 131072-token cache contract without reducing the prior advertised capability. Capacity evidence validates physical paged-cache allocation and the final logical position, not a single 131072-token prefill invocation. Public logical sequence lengths remain unaligned: internal tile/page padding and 128-token active-expert chunking are not exposed. Exact prefill executes 4*S token-specific expert routes, but current sparse matmul allocates a physical 32-row tile to every route; this is a performance limitation, not a context-capacity limit.

prefill.capacity_status: paged-cache capacity validated at 131072; full-length prefill compute was not run
prefill.cache_behavior: fills local four-KV-head paged key/value caches through an arbitrary page-table mapping and returns the exact logical sequence length
prefill.expert_behavior: pads internally to 32, chunks at 128, executes exactly four token-specific active experts through three sparse matmuls, all-reduces the TP-local down result, and slices back to logical S

decode.cache_behavior: updates the TP-local physical page selected by the replicated page table; eager and traced physical cache pages are bit-identical on both ranks
decode.decode_layout: 30-core [32,96] QKV input shard, 40-core [32,64] QKV output shard, 10-core input/post-attention RMSNorms, replicated layer output
